race.name: Ski North Ultra
race.tagline: One hundred kilometers of classic-only skiing along Minnesota's North Shore of Lake Superior — one of North America's few 100K XC ski races, launched in 2025.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations along the North Shore trail systems

climate.description: March on Minnesota's North Shore means reliable deep snow influenced by Lake Superior's moderating effect. Temperatures range from -15°C to 5°C. The lake effect provides consistent snowfall while the March date brings lengthening days. North Shore terrain is hilly — the course climbs from lake level up through the Sawtooth Mountains.
challenges: Hilly North Shore terrain with significant elevation gain
Lake Superior weather can change rapidly
March conditions range from deep cold to spring thaw
Remote North Shore location — limited services

course.primary: A 100km classic-only ultra-distance race along Minnesota's North Shore of Lake Superior, using the Norpine, Cascade River State Park, and Bally Creek trail systems. Hilly terrain through boreal forest with views of Lake Superior. One of North America's very few 100km XC ski races. Classic technique only — no skating permitted.
course.surface: Machine-groomed North Shore trail systems through boreal forest
course.grooming: Norpine, Cascade River State Park, and Bally Creek trail systems
features: 100km ultra distance — one of North America's few 100K XC ski races
Minnesota's North Shore of Lake Superior setting
Norpine, Cascade River State Park, and Bally Creek trail systems

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum distance score with the 100km ultra option. Strong technicality on hilly North Shore terrain. Lake Superior scenery is exceptional. Very new event (founded 2025) with small field limits prestige and competitive depth.

history.summary: Ski North Ultra launched in 2025 as one of North America's very few 100km cross-country ski races. Set along Minnesota's iconic North Shore of Lake Superior, the classic-only event uses the Norpine, Cascade River State Park, and Bally Creek trail systems. The 100km distance fills a gap in the North American citizen racing calendar for ultra-endurance classic skiers seeking distances comparable to European ultra events.
notable_facts: Founded in 2025 — one of North America's newest ski marathons
100km ultra distance — extremely rare in North American XC skiing
Classic technique only — no skating permitted
Set along Minnesota's North Shore of Lake Superior
Uses three trail systems: Norpine, Cascade River, and Bally Creek
Fills a gap for ultra-distance classic skiing in North America
